activities_projects.title: Election Result Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand how a majority is calculated to form a government
materials: Hypothetical data of 5 different political parties
steps: Take a state with 68 constituencies (like Himachal Pradesh in the text)
Distribute the 68 seats among Party A, B, C, and Independents
Calculate the 'Majority Mark' (Half of 68 + 1)
Determine which party becomes the Ruling Party and which becomes the Opposition
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the numerical requirement for forming a stable government
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in identifying majority and opposition roles based on numbers (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: MLA vs. Minister Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Distinguish between legislative and executive roles
steps: List the duties of an MLA (representing a constituency, debating in the house)
List the duties of a Minister (heading a department, implementing laws)
Fill a Venn Diagram showing where these roles overlap
Explain the process of how an MLA becomes a Minister
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Clarifies the difference between the legislature and the executive branch
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of comparison in the Venn Diagram (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Legislative Assembly Debate Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the process of questioning and accountability
materials: Role cards (Speaker, Opposition MLA, Health Minister)
Textbook transcript of the assembly debate
steps: Assign roles based on the Patalpuram incident discussed in the chapter
The Opposition MLA must ask a sharp question about a local problem (e.g., water shortage)
The Minister must respond with data on 'Government Action' (e.g., water tankers sent)
The Speaker must moderate the time for each speaker
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates how the government is held accountable by elected representatives
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of the arguments and adherence to assembly decorum (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Governor’s Executive Role
activities_projects.objective: Examine the appointment and status of the Governor
steps: Research why the Governor is appointed by the Central Government and not elected
List the major functions: Appointing the CM, signing bills, ensuring constitutional rule
Compare the Governor's role in the State to the President's role in the Union
Find the name and party background of the current Governor of your state
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the constitutional hierarchy of the state government
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity in explaining the Governor's relationship with the CM and Center (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Press Conference Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate how the government communicates with the public
materials: A newspaper report on a recent government announcement
steps: Identify who held the press conference and what the main announcement was
List the questions asked by the journalists as mentioned in the report
Analyze why a press conference is held after an assembly session
Discuss if the government's response was satisfactory based on the news report
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the role of the media as a bridge between government and citizens
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical thinking in analyzing the transparency of government communication (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Government Departments Flowchart
activities_projects.objective: Trace the path of decision-making in various departments
steps: Pick three departments (e.g., Public Works, Agriculture, Education)
Draw a hierarchy: CM $ightarrow$ Cabinet Minister $ightarrow$ Secretary $ightarrow$ Local Officials
Write one specific responsibility for each department based on the chapter
Explain how these departments carry out the promises made in the Assembly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the administrative structure of the state executive
activities_projects.evaluation: Correctness of the hierarchy and departmental functions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Coalition Government Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Understand what happens when no party gets a majority
steps: Create a scenario where three parties have 30%, 25%, and 15% of seats (Total 100)
Let students negotiate to form a 'Coalition Government'
List two problems a coalition government might face compared to a single-party majority
Define the term 'Coalition' in the context of state politics
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explores the complexities of power-sharing and political alliances
activities_projects.evaluation: Successful negotiation and reasoning for the alliance (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Constituency Problem Survey
activities_projects.objective: Relate textbook concepts to real-life local governance
steps: Identify the name of your local MLA and their political party
List three major problems in your constituency (e.g., roads, sanitation, electricity)
Find out if the MLA has raised these issues in the Legislative Assembly
Draft a 50-word letter to the MLA requesting action on one specific problem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects the student to their representative and local public issues
activities_projects.evaluation: Identification of local issues and formal structure of the letter (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Speaker's Authority
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of the neutral head of the house
steps: Research how the Speaker of the Legislative Assembly is elected
List three powers the Speaker has during a heated debate
Explain why it is important for the Speaker to be impartial (neutral)
Identify who the current Speaker is in your state assembly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the importance of rules and neutrality in legislative proceedings
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of the Speaker's powers and selection process (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Wall Newspaper: The Patalpuram Inquiry
activities_projects.objective: Synthesize multiple viewpoints on a public issue
steps: Create a section for 'The People’s Voices' (Protests and Rallies)
Create a section for 'The Opposition’s Questions' (Criticism)
Create a section for 'The Government’s Response' (Decisions and Actions)
Design a headline that summarizes the entire health crisis of Patalpuram
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops a holistic understanding of how democracy processes a crisis
activities_projects.evaluation: Inclusion of all three perspectives and creative presentation (10 marks)
